title: Ackermann Four-Wheel Steering Geometry
content: Ackermann steering geometry ensures all four wheels follow concentric arcs during a turn, eliminating tyre scrub. For front-only steering: cot(θ_outer) − cot(θ_inner) = W/L. For four-wheel steering (4WS), rear wheels steer opposite (low speed) or same direction (high speed) as front.
extended_content: 4WS modes: 1) Counter-phase rear steering (low speed, < 35 km/h) — reduces turning radius by up to 30%, minimum turning radius R_min ≈ L/(sin θ_max). 2) In-phase rear steering (high speed, > 60 km/h) — improves lane-change stability, rear steer angle 1–3°. Neutral steer point (NSP) control: in-phase rear steer moves NSP rearward, reducing yaw overshoot. Rear steering angle δ_r = k·δ_f (k negative counter-phase, positive in-phase). Rack and pinion + rear servo actuator common implementation. Electro-hydraulic rear actuator response < 100 ms.
units: δ: rad, W: m (track), L: m (wheelbase), R: m
source: Milliken & Milliken, Race Car Vehicle Dynamics, SAE 1995; Abe, Vehicle Handling Dynamics, 2015; Honda 4WS Patent JP60-61373
